mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between retail sales growth and housing starts indicates shifting consumer spending patterns; when retail sales rise while housing starts decline, equity markets price in sustained consumption demand, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01, or returns are negative in >60% of cases when the divergence condition holds.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during late-cycle regimes, or 5-day forward returns show negative mean when INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor market expansion, which supports aggregate income growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll changes fail to predict positive returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth combined with low federal funds rates creates a favorable macro environment where robust economic expansion is not offset by financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative returns in >58% of cases, or the interaction effect disappears when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 6.0 percent marks a severe labor market deterioration that triggers recession risk pricing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.
